critical_claims.proposition: The proposed tutor will improve Algebra I outcomes for this district's ninth-grade students.
The $57,600 license price fits the stated $65,000 budget before training and support costs.
The proposed data-retention terms satisfy district privacy, consent, and records obligations.
Teachers have enough capacity and a defined process to handle tutor errors and escalations safely.
A 600-student launch would improve rather than widen access for students with different language, disability, device, and connectivity needs.
The district should launch to 600 students before a smaller controlled pilot and privacy, accessibility, procurement, and workload reviews.

common_false_claims.claim: The vendor's curriculum-alignment statement proves improved student outcomes.
common_false_claims.reason: Alignment and causal learning impact are different claims; no local or independent outcome evidence is supplied.

common_false_claims.claim: De-identified student conversations create no privacy or governance risk.
common_false_claims.reason: The proposal still requires jurisdiction- and policy-specific review of collection, retention, access, and consent.
That is license cost only; training, support, accessibility, and administration costs are not estimated.

source_pack.title: Authored district baseline and vendor-proposal summary

acceptable_unresolved_outcomes.reason: No independent or local outcome evidence is supplied.
Required privacy and records reviews are incomplete.
Teacher escalation workload and operating process are not measured.
Accessibility, language, device, and connectivity impacts are not evaluated.
